human_verification.notes: Reviewed the housing resident relations package for boundary quality, authority-source quality, resident-sensitive evidence handling, complaint and exception handling, fair housing, VAWA, privacy, inspection, grievance, tenant participation, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to resident relations in housing, real estate development, and community development with lease, unit, work-order, inspection, resident complaint, grievance, resident association, fair housing, VAWA, privacy, and policy-aware service handling.

Authority sources cover HUD multifamily occupancy, HUD management-agent resident communication, resident rights, public housing grievance rules, tenant participation, fair housing, VAWA confidentiality, Privacy Act/EIV handling, NSPIRE inspection posture, and service coordination handoff boundaries with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal resident maintenance status, missing details, policy exceptions, unsupported workarounds, repeat failures, reasonable accommodations, VAWA emergency transfer confidentiality, public housing grievances, tenant participation terminology, low-confidence handling, conflicting policies, and tenant-specific adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/housing-real-estate-development-and-community-development/resident-relations-specialist/ to keep this overlay in the canonical NAICS-derived path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around legal, fair housing, accommodation, VAWA, grievance, rent, subsidy, maintenance, inspection, safety, financial, and policy-override authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated housing resident relations overlay where resident-sensitive service, complaint, communication, and escalation workflows must be grounded in housing records.

The specialist is intentionally support-oriented: it knows when to hand work to property management, leasing, affordable housing compliance, fair housing, legal, maintenance, inspections, resident services, rent and subsidy, grievance, safety, and executive approval lanes.
